RAHIM, Shaikh Mo- hamed ; 6. Olpad: dist. Surat, 1884 ; educ Olpad and Bulsar Came to South Africa carly in 1900, Settled in Cape Town, engaging in commerce as general dealer, wholesale fruit- erer, etc. Public work Has taken active part in all matters affecting Indians and other non- Europeans—social, ed ucational, municipal and Parliamentary. As soon as he arrived. he recognised need. for some elementary educa tion among the Indian commanity. He. started a night school at his own expense and founded other similar institutions, Was among first to see the strength of an All-Union Indian Organisation, drawing Mr. Gandhi's attention to the fact in 1907. Sub- sequently he helped in the establishment of what is now the S.A.L.C, Sponsored other conferences in the Cape to oppose anti-Indian measures in 1916 and other dates. ‘Took prominent part in consolidating the position of Indian traders as to their licences ‘Asa public man he has come into contact with members fof the Government and Legislature. He saw need of an Indian Agency in South Africa and submitted a statement to this effect to the Paddison Deputation in 1925. “Has appeared before numerous commissions and was thanked by the Paddison and other deputations for his information on Indian questions. Had consistently opposed the condonation and other schemes, which he considered would reduce the Indian population. Is secty. of the Bababia Muslim Soc. (charitable body) : presdt.. 220 THE SOUTH AFRICAN SANNYASI, Bha-wani Dayal; Com-missioner for Oaths ; 6. Johannesburg. Sept. 10; 1892; s, of late Jairam ‘Singh, ex- presdt. Transvaal In- dian Assoen. ; educ. at Bihar. Leaving South Africa 1904, returned 1912. On return elected presdt. Young Men's Indian Assocn., Ger- miston, 1913; ‘one of the organisers of the Indian Strike at New- castle in 1913 and was sentenced to term of three months” im- prisonment with hard labour, with his wife Jag-rani Devi and child Ramdutt. Edited Hindi section of The Indian Opinion in 1914, Dharma Vir 1917-18, Aryavarta 1931-32. Organised the first and second South African Hindi Literary Conferences in Lady- smith and Pietermaritzburg in 1916 and 1917 respec- tively. Elected a member of Bijolia-Mewar (Rajputana) Commission to investigate grievances of the passive resisters in 1920. Attended and participated in many other political, social, literary and religious conferences during his short stay in India, Founded Jag-rani Press at Jacobs in 1922, Edited, printed and published the weekly Hindi in Hindi and English from 1922 to 1925. Repstd. South African Indians at Indian National Congress, Amritsar 1919, and Gaya 1922 and was instrumental in getting The S.A.1.C. affli- ated to The Indian National Congress. Elected a member of the South African Indian Deputation to India in 1925. Established the Pravasi-Bhawan consisting of a fine library and free school at Bahuara, Khargarh near Sasaram (Bihar) in 1926. Admitted into the Order of Sannyasis in 1927. Acted as a representative of the S.A. Indian Congress to study the working of the New Immigration Act in the districts of Utrecht and Vryheid rendering valuable assistance. Elected presdt. of the First Indian Overseas Conference held at Brindaban (Muttra) in 1930. Elected presdt. of Shahabad District Congress Committee and was sentenced to simple imprisonment for two and a half years under section 124 (a) of Indian Penal Code for delivering seditious speecnes. Placed in Class A and served his term in Hazaribagh Gaol. Released in 1931 unconditionally by amnesty after serving one year. During the period edited a hand-written monthly magazine Karagar in the Gaol. Published “a report on the emigrants repatriated to India under the assisted emigration scheme from South Africa and on the protlem of returned emigrants from all colonies’” jointly with Pandit Benarsidas Chaturvedi in 1931 and subsequently published the “public opinion on the assisted emigration scheme under Indo- South African Agreement.” Presided at the All-india Hindi Journalists’ Conference at the Senate Hall, Calcutta University in 1931, Presided at tenth Bihar Provincial Hindi Literary "Conference at Baidyanathdham (Deoghar) in 1931. Author of many Hindi books, namely : History of Passive Resistance in South Africa, My Experiences of South Africa, Story of My Prison-life, Biography of Mahatma ‘Gandhi, Indians in Transvaal, Natalian Hindu, Vedic ReligionINDIAN WHO'S WHO and Aryan Culture, Educator and Cultivator, The Vedic Prayer, etc. Elected Honorary Indian Probation Officer ; v.-presdt., N.LC. since 1921. Founder of many political, sociat, religious and literary societies both in South’ Africa’and India. Single-handed con- ducted, in India, strenuous agitation against proposed segregation measure by Union Govt, 1939: was able to carry the whole country with him, from H.E. the Viceroy to the peasant.
